stem: A decision-maker must choose between two options. Option A (the highway route) yields a guaranteed return of $175K. Option B (the scenic route) depends on traffic conditions: with probability 41-60% (uncertain), it yields $279K; otherwise it yields $60K. Compute the expected value of each option and determine which is better. How does the uncertainty in traffic conditions affect the recommendation?

ground_truth.answer: EV(A) = $175K (fixed). EV(B) ranges from $149.8K to $191.4K. Point estimate: $171.7K. Option A is preferred at most assumptions (EV_A > EV_B when P(good) < 52.5%).

solution_steps: The decision switches at P(good) = 52.5%, which falls within the uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Picking a single probability and giving a definitive recommendation without sensitivity analysis
Not computing the breakeven probability where the optimal decision switches
Ignoring the range of outcomes and focusing only on expected values (risk-neutral assumption)
